title: @koylanai: The best way to improve an agent’s skills is to treat their execution history as...
rawContent:
Tweet by @koylanai (Muratcan Koylan)
Author bio: Member of Technical Staff, Research @sullyai | prev: AI Agent Systems Manager, 99Ravens | Context Engineer | Builder, creator, optimist...
Text: The best way to improve an agent’s skills is to treat their execution history as training data.

- Skills are distilled playbooks grounded in real runs, merged holistically, with failure analysis in the loop.

- Prefer one strong, deduplicated skill tree over many tiny conflicting docs.

- Smaller models can still contribute to skill evolution.

Trace2Skill (Qwen team) takes a pool of agent trajectories like their successes and failures, dispatches parallel analyst agents to extract lessons from each one independently, then merges everything into a single evolved skill document. 

A 35B model's evolved skills improved a 122B model by up to 57.65 percentage points on WikiTQ. Skills evolved on one task domain (spreadsheets) transferred to a completely different domain (Wikipedia QA).
